D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Auto WB Mixed-Light Failure

a single global gain can't make daylight and tungsten patches both neutral at once — AWB always picks a compromise that leaves one half wrong

Automatic WB estimates one gain triple for the whole frame. In mixed light — daylight from a window plus tungsten table lamp — that single gain cannot make the daylight side and the tungsten side both neutral. Whichever side AWB locks onto, the other side keeps its cast.

The scene is split: left half lit by 5500 K daylight, right half lit by 3000 K tungsten. Drag the area slider to change which side dominates the gray-world estimate. The picked gain is applied uniformly — watch the “wrong” side stay cast while the “chosen” side becomes neutral.
